note: Sprint 5.13: adds Useful Compute benchmark ledger v0.1. Measures local deterministic work units and optional benchmark reports for reward modeling. No automatic payout, no wallet access, no network, no consensus. scripts/trinity/useful_compute_benchmark.py runs deterministic micro-benchmarks per (backend, task_type) pair; benchmark_id is stable across workers/machines for same inputs; wall_time_seconds is real but does not contaminate the id; worker_id is stored only as sha16 hash. Reward model gains optional benchmark inputs and applies backend-kind policy: placeholder benchmark zeros the reward; sandbox_toy benchmark keeps the reward but forces manual_review; real_backend (reserved) forces manual review until enabled. Worker gains --benchmark-report flag; result + reward emit benchmark_id, normalized_work_score, benchmark_source even when absent (benchmark_source=none). Result schema bumped v0.3 to v0.4. Pending reward schema bumped v0.2 to v0.3. Validator + governance gate updated to enforce v0.4 result + v0.3 reward respectively. Web console adds benchmark + work_score columns to per-task table; three new counters (unbenchmarked_rewards, benchmarked_rewards, experimental_rewards); disclaimer Benchmark score is not proof of useful scientific output; export_summary includes benchmark_source_counts. Tests: 56 new across tests/trinity/test_{useful_compute_benchmark,useful_compute_benchmark_schema,useful_compute_reward_with_benchmark,trinity_benchmark_web_console}.py; existing schema tests updated for v0.4/v0.3; static safety + no-network checks extended to cover useful_compute_benchmark.py. Trinity suite 496/496 PASS with materials-engine-private present. Badge bumped to v0.7 (worker + replay + governance + daemon + console + backend adapters + benchmark ledger). docs/TRINITY_USEFUL_COMPUTE_BENCHMARK_LEDGER_V01.md. No consensus, RPC, node, miner, wallet or wire-format change.
